arts.ttl: Mark Robins takes old fashioned approach to tackle 'strange' Stoke City fitness problem
arts.sum: Mark Robins is reviewing how best to manage Stoke City’s fitness levels in the short and long-term after seeing players go down with cramp at the weekend. But that doesn’t necessarily help in the here and now as Stoke try to win games under Robins to haul themselves up the Championship table and away from danger.

arts.ttl: ‘Still in trouble’ – Bristol City vs Stoke City prediction: The72
arts.sum: The72's writers offer their Bristol City vs Stoke City prediction ahead of the Championship clash on Wednesday. The post ‘Still in trouble’ – Bristol City vs Stoke City prediction: The72 appeared first on The72 - Football League News.

arts.ttl: Brian Tinnion interview notebook: Bristol City plan, Mayulu future, Semenyo sell-on and more
arts.sum: Brian Tinnion shed some additional light on this deal as he confirmed the Robins had secured a loan fee for the striker's services and also suggested Sturm Graz are covering all of Mayulu's wages, while also reinforcing the Reds' plan to look at his development come the end of the season. With the January transfer window now in the rear view mirror, questions surrounded a whole host of topics including the Reds' recent squad update, summer and winter transfer decisions, Nahki Wells' future in the West Country and a whole lot more.

arts.ttl: Bristol City verdict as January transfer risks to be tested after Swansea loss
arts.sum: That will worry most supporters considering the fact there are still 15 games to be played, but before the transfer window closed, Manning insisted that City had not left themselves short of options. I'm sure most of you will be eager to banish Sunday's game to the back of your mind and instead look forward to Wednesday night's clash with Stoke, but here are the main talking points from the Reds' 1-0 defeat to Swansea City...

arts.ttl: How Stoke City could vote down fiercest rivals as old boy Verlinden sees red in new setback
arts.sum: Stoke City are likely to be asked to vote on plans for three-team promotion from the National League - and as the outcome will also dictate relegation from League Two, they could inadvertently be having a huge say on the future of their biggest rivals. The report adds: “All 72 National League clubs - including the likes of Oldham, Altrincham, Southend and Hartlepool - have backed a letter to the EFL asking for an extra promotion place into League Two from next season.
